english: čém(s) (chems) °jóg-pa ('jog pa) to deposit a testamentary disposal or devise for a son Glr.

1. the noise made by thunder, by the shock of an earthquake etc., °brúg-sgra ('brug sgra) čem-čém (chem chem) sgrógs-pa (sgrogs pa) the rolling, roaring, clapping of thunder; bžad-gád (bzhad gad) °ur ('ur) čém-pa (chem pa) a roaring laughter. — 2. kra (khra) čem-čém (chem chem) v. krá-bo (khra bo).

termin. of če (che), čer (cher) °gró-ba ('gro ba) to grow, increase; čer (cher) skyé-ba (skye ba) to become great; to grow up, čer (cher) skyés-pa (skyes pa) partic. grown up, adult; da-dúṅ (da dung) čer (cher) toṅ (tong) go on! go on! Mil.; rgyal-sríd (rgyal srid) byá-ba (bya ba) čer (cher) ma (ma) byuṅ (byung) Glr. his government was not (yet) of much consequence (as he was too young); ṅés-pa (nges pa) čer (cher) med (med) this is not quite evident to me Mil.; perh. Tar. 36, 16; 101, 22; 120, 21; 169, 14 will allow a similar interpretation of čer (cher). — čér-na (cher na) S.O. yea; still more (?).

1. instrum. of če (che). — 2. pf. of čé-ba (che ba), as adv. very, ṅa-zás (nga zas) ṅan (ngan) čés-kyi (ches kyi) as the food is very bad Dzl.; čes (ches) sgrín-pa (sgrin pa) very prudent or clever Sch.; čes (ches) dár-bar (dar bar) gyúr-to (gyur to) it spread very much Tar.

1. pf. of čé-ba (che ba) to be great, ha-čaṅ (ha cang) yaṅ (yang) čes-so (ches so) he is much too great Dzl.; dmag-dguṅ (dmag dgung) čes-pa (ches pa) a great army; dbaṅ (dbang) čes-pas (ches pas) being very mighty Glr.; čar (char) čes-pas (ches pas) as it rained heavily Pth.; dga (dga') čes-nas (ches nas) greatly rejoicing Mil. — 2. to believe, but only when preceded by yid (yid) (resp. ťugs (thugs)), or bden (bden) (col.), c. la (la), also c. accus., or par (par), that, Dzl. 2S, 18.

1. num. fig.: 126. — 2. as a word for itself seldom to be met with, e.g. Ld.-Glr. Schl. fol. 13, 6; Tar. 129, 20; signification not clearly to be made out. *čó-med-pa* (*cho med pa*) C. = dón-med-pa (don med pa) to no purpose, vain; fickle.

the way or method of doing a thing, e.g. of solving an arithmetical problem Wdk., of curing maladies S.g., esp. used of magic performances, čó-ga-pa (cho ga pa) Cs., čó-ga-mkán (cho ga mkhan) Mil., a performer of such ceremonies. Whether it may safely be used for religious rites or ceremonies in general, is doubtful.

lamentation, wailing, esp. lamentations for the dead, dirge, °débs-pa ('debs pa) Dzl., °bód-pa ('bod pa), °dón-pa ('don pa) in more recent literature, byéd-pa (byed pa) Sch., to lament, wail, cry, clamour; with la (la) to cry to a person; the crying of a new-born child Thgy.
magical trick, jugglery, often put to rdzu-°pŕúl (rdzu 'phrul), also used of the apparitions and doings of goblins Mil. Cf. rdzu-°pŕúl (rdzu 'phrul).
to set on (a dog), čó-čó-ba (cho cho ba) to set on repeatedly Cs.

Mil.; Cs.: the mother's family or lineage; čo-rígs (cho rigs) Dzl. frq. Cs.: 1. the father's lineage, descent by the father's side; 2. an honourable extraction. — čo-rís (cho ris) Mil., frq. = čo-rígs (cho rigs), also applied to things, e.g. a cane: čo-rís (cho ris) yé-nas (ye nas) btsún-pa (btsun pa) a cane of an excellent kind, not coming from any mean or noxious plant.

1. die, dice, game at dice. — 2. seal (?) — čó-lo-mkán (cho lo mkhan) a dice-player Cs., čó-lo (cho lo) rtsé-ba (rtse ba) to play at dice Cs.; čó-lo-ris (cho lo ris) Glr. the figure of a die, a square figure, in Glr. 47, 9 the Mongol translation substitutes a wheel, v. °kór-lo ('khor lo); a checkered colouring or pattern, e.g. of cotton cloth C.
1. for čó-ga (cho ga); bón-čog (bon chog) Mil. the ceremony of the Bonpos. — 2. v. gčóg-pa (gchog pa). — 3. v. čóg-pa (chog pa).

vb., sbst., adj. 1. to be sufficient, sufficiency, sufficient, c. dat: ṅéd-la (nged la) dé-kas (de kas) čóg (chog) it is sufficient for us, we are satisfied Mil.; drís-pas (dris pas) (instr. of paí (pa'i)) čóg-go (chog go) Dzl. 2S, 10 (there has been) enough of asking, = don't ask any more! gáṅ-du (gang du) bžúgs (bzhugs) kyaṅ (kyang) čóg-par (chog par) °dúg ('dug) it is sufficient (for him) wherever he may live, i.e. he is satisfied with any place of living Mil.; ṅéd-la (nged la) nor (nor) loṅs-spyód-kyis (longs spyod kyis) čóg-pa (chog pa) yod (yod) we have money and goods enough Mil.; °dí ('di) gsúm-gyis (gsum gyis) čóg-na (chog na) if these three are sufficient for you Mil.; rín-po-čes (rin po ches) čóg-par (chog par)
